1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

43	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` — RE-ATTEMPT iter-076 rollbacks
44	
45	**File status.** 7 syntactic sorries; **file compiles cleanly** (0 errors). Sorries: L122 `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf`, L207 `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`, L232 `cotangentExactSeqBeta`, L266 `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (absorbs former L604 `h_exact` + L617 `h_epi`), L550 `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega`, L567 `cotangent_at_section`, L703 `serre_duality_genus`.
46	
47	**Goal.** Close `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` (L207) and/or `cotangentExactSeqBeta` (L232) — and ideally also `cotangentExactSeq_structure` (L266) by re-enabling the preserved `/- ITER-076 disabled chain -/` block. The L122 / L550 / L567 / L703 sorries are out of scope for this lane.
48	
49	**Re-attempt recipe** (each error individually addressed):
50	
51	- **`cotangentExactSeqAlpha` (L207).** Five specific fixes from the iter-076 error inventory:
52	  1. **L244** (additivity of the `d_target.d` AddMonoidHom). Replace `(by intro a b; simp)` with `by intro a b; rw [(f.c.app U).hom.map_add, D_X.d_add]`.
53	  2. **L248** (`d_mul`). Insert explicit `show D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom (a * b)) = a • D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b) + b • D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom a)` *before* the `rw`. Then `rw [(f.c.app U).hom.map_mul, D_X.d_mul]; rfl`.
54	  3. **L256** (`d_map`). Type-annotate the `h` binder in `congr_arg`: `have := congr_arg (fun h : _ ⟶ _ => (ConcreteCategory.hom h) x) (f.c.naturality f')` (or pre-bind `let h_eq := f.c.naturality f'`).
55	  4. **L258** — same shape as L248: insert explicit `show` to commit the `(f.c.app V).hom (Y.presheaf.map f' x)` form.
56	  5. **L266** (`d_app`'s `hcomm` step). The `rfl` claim for `φ_g' ≫ f.c = adj_f.unit ≫ (pushforward).map φ_fg'` fails — this is the adjunction-coherence identity, **not definitional**. Derive via `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right` + `Equiv.symm_apply_eq`. *If non-trivial in-iteration*: roll back just the `d_app` block to `sorry` (preserve the rest of `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`).
57	
58	- **`cotangentExactSeqBeta` (L232).** Recipe:
59	  - Prepend `set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in` to the `noncomputable def`.
60	  - Remove the redundant `exact h4` at the former L332 (the prior `simp only` / `erw [h6]` chain already closes the goal).
61	  - Or inline `η` rather than `set`-ing it, to reduce elaboration cost.
62	
63	- **`cotangentExactSeq_structure` (L266).** Once `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`/`Beta` compile, re-enable the preserved `/- ITER-076 disabled chain -/` block (Steps 1–7 of `h_zero` with `hα_fac`/`hβ_fac`/`hd_app`/`hcoh`). The `unfold cotangentExactSeqAlpha; unfold cotangentExactSeqBeta` invocations inside that block depend on the upstream defs compiling — once they do, the block should close `_structure.h_zero` (and the formerly-separate `h_exact`/`h_epi` route reduces to `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` + `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` + `PresheafOfModules.epi_iff_surjective`).

73	### 2. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — RE-ATTEMPT iter-076 rollbacks
74	
75	**File status.** 7 syntactic sorries; **file compiles cleanly** (0 errors). Sorries: L495, L819, L847 (3 dead-end substeps — stay), L1077 `h_diff_pi_smul_f`, L1106 `f_R.map_smul'`, L1116 `g_R.map_smul'`, L1145 `h_loc_exact`.
76	
77	**Goal.** Close one or more of `f_R.map_smul'` (L1106), `g_R.map_smul'` (L1116), `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1077), and downstream `h_loc_exact` (L1145). The L495/L819/L847 dead-ends are out of scope.
78	
79	**Re-attempt recipe**. The iter-076 root-cause analysis identified **instance literality** as the hard blocker: `h_mod_X_i` was constructed via `convert h` (and `convert h using N`), producing a `Module R scK₀.X_i` instance that differs from the canonical `e_i.toAddEquiv.module R` by residual eta-congruence. As a result, in `f_R.map_smul'`/`g_R.map_smul'` the `congr 1` step for `r • x = e_i.symm (r • e_i x)` cannot close by `rfl`.
80	
81	Additionally, for X₃: `(ComplexShape.up ℕ).next n` does **NOT** reduce to `n + 1` by `rfl` (opaque `Classical.choose`); use `CochainComplex.next` (Mathlib lemma: `(ComplexShape.up α).next i = i + 1`). The iter-076 working pattern for `h_mod_X₃`:
82	```lean
83	have h_mod_X₃ : Module R scK₀.X₃ := by
84	  have h_eq : scK₀.X₃ = K₀.X (n + 1) := by
85	    show K₀.X ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).next n) = K₀.X (n + 1)
86	    rw [CochainComplex.next]
87	  rw [h_eq]
88	  dsimp [K₀, cechCochain, cechComplexFunctor, toModuleKSheaf, toModuleKPresheaf_obj]
89	  letI := h_mod_pi₃
90	  have h : Module R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₃) := e₃.toAddEquiv.module R
91	  convert h
92	```
93	This pattern fixes the L952 error, but the trailing `convert h` still leaves the instance non-literal. To make `f_R.map_smul'`/`g_R.map_smul'` close by `rfl`, **bypass `convert`** entirely: use explicit `Eq.mpr` casting on a manual type-level equality `↑scK₀.X_i = ↑(∏ᶜ Z_i)` so the instance is *literally* `e_i.toAddEquiv.module R`.
94	
95	Steps (in order):
96	1. **Rebuild `h_mod_X_i`** via explicit `Eq.mpr`/`cast` instead of `convert h`. For X₃, prepend a `CochainComplex.next` rewrite (as shown). The aim: `(Module R scK₀.X_i).toSMul` equals `e_i.toEquiv.smul R` *literally*.
97	2. **With literal `h_mod_X_i` in hand**, the iter-072 `calc` chains in `f_R.map_smul'` / `g_R.map_smul'` work as written (the `congr 1` for `r • x = e_i.symm (r • e_i x)` will close by `rfl` because the smul comes from `e_i.toEquiv.smul R`).
98	3. **Then `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1077)**: attack via the documented S1–S8 recipe (decomposition of the 5-layer functor stack into per-summand R-linearity). The preserved comment block at L1077 documents the recipe.
99	4. **`h_loc_exact` (L1145)**: downstream — closes by `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` (or `exact_of_localized_span`) once the per-summand chain compiles.
100	
101	**Note: `h_diff_pi_smul_g`** declaration was DROPPED iter-076 (un-typeable). **DO NOT re-add it.** If `g_R.map_smul'` needs an analogue of `h_diff_pi_smul_f` for the g-side, either reformulate the statement to avoid the `e₃`-codomain mismatch (use a `change`/`convert` inside the proof body) or split `g_R.map_smul'` so it doesn't need a free-standing `h_diff_pi_smul_g`.

111	### 3. `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` — REPAIR compilation (NEW PRIORITY)
112	
113	**File status.** 1 syntactic sorry (L177 `nonempty_jacobianWitness` — protected by `nonempty_jacobianWitness`'s deferral to Phase C step C3); **file does NOT compile** — 6 errors, latent since archon[073/prover]:
114	
115	1. **L59 col 6**: `unexpected token 'ι'; expected '_' or identifier` inside `def IsAlbanese ... := ∃ (ι : C ⟶ J), ...`. Likely a parser-recovery from an upstream elaboration issue in the L57 `def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C) (J : Over (Spec (.of k))) [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop`. Hypothesis: the outer `variable {k : Type u} [Field k]` is not auto-binding through the `namespace IsAlbanese` boundary, leaving `k` unresolved.
116	2. **L67 col 43** and **L71 col 43**: `typeclass instance problem is stuck — Field ?m.38`. Both occur in `noncomputable def ofCurve {C P J} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J` (and the analogous `lemma comp_ofCurve`). The implicit `{C P J}` doesn't declare `k`, and the outer `variable {k : Type u} [Field k]` is not propagating. 6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus

8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The Jacobian is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness
16	(`JacobianWitness`) for the curve `C`. Existence of such a witness is the single
17	remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C scaffolding (`nonempty_jacobianWitness`);
18	the four protected instances on `Jacobian C` all project from the witness directly.
19	
20	The file contains:
21	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
22	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
23	- `JacobianWitness`: a bundled candidate Albanese object together with the universal
24	  property uniformly over the choice of $k$-rational marked point.
25	- `nonempty_jacobianWitness`: the (deferred) existence of such a witness — the single
26	  Phase-C sorry, absorbing both higher-genus Albanese existence and genus-`0` rigidity.
27	- `Jacobian` and its four protected instances, each obtained by projection from the
28	  Albanese witness.
29	
30	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
31	
32	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
33	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
34	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
35	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
36	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`. The witness-based definition avoids this
37	issue: in genus `0` the witness's underlying scheme `J` is `Spec k`, and in genus `> 0`
38	the witness's `J` is the honest Albanese object.
39	-/
40	
41	set_option autoImplicit false
42	
43	universe u
44	
45	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
46	
47	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
48	
49	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
50	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
51	  [IsProper C.hom]
52	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
53	
54	/-- The Albanese universal property for a pointed smooth proper curve `(C, P)`.
55	An object `J` (with abelian-variety structure) is the Albanese of `(C, P)` if it
56	receives a universal pointed morphism `C ⟶ J` sending `P` to the identity. -/
57	def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C) (J : Over (Spec (.of k)))
58	    [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop :=
59	  ∃ (ι : C ⟶ J), P ≫ ι = η[J] ∧
60	    ∀ {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
61	      [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]),
62	      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = ι ≫ g
63	
64	namespace IsAlbanese
65	
66	noncomputable def ofCurve {C P J} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
67	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J :=
68	  Classical.choose h
69	
70	lemma comp_ofCurve {C P J} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
71	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) :
72	    P ≫ h.ofCurve = η[J] :=
73	  (Classical.choose_spec h).1
74	
75	lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp {C P J} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
76	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J)
77	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
78	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
79	    ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = h.ofCurve ≫ g :=
80	  (Classical.choose_spec h).2 f hf
81	
82	/-- The Albanese object is unique up to a unique isomorphism compatible with the
83	universal morphisms. -/
84	theorem unique {C : Over (Spec (.of k))} {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C}
85	    {J₁ J₂ : Over (Spec (.of k))}
86	    [GrpObj J₁] [IsProper J₁.hom] [Smooth J₁.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₁.hom]
87	    [GrpObj J₂] [IsProper J₂.hom] [Smooth J₂.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₂.hom]
88	    (h₁ : IsAlbanese C P J₁) (h₂ : IsAlbanese C P J₂) :
89	    ∃! (e : J₁ ⟶ J₂), h₂.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ e := by
90	  have ⟨g, hg_eq, hg_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
91	  have ⟨h, hh_eq, hh_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
92	  have ⟨g₁, hg₁_eq, hg₁_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
93	  have g₁_eq_id : g₁ = 𝟙 J₁ := by
94	    refine (hg₁_unique (𝟙 J₁) ?_).symm
95	    simp
96	  have ggh_eq_g₁ : g ≫ h = g₁ := by
97	    refine (hg₁_unique (g ≫ h) ?_).symm
98	    rw [Category.assoc, hg_eq, hh_eq]
99	  have hgh : g ≫ h = 𝟙 J₁ := by rw [ggh_eq_g₁, g₁_eq_id]
100	  have ⟨h₂, hh₂_eq, hh₂_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
101	  have h₂_eq_id : h₂ = 𝟙 J₂ := by
102	    refine (hh₂_unique (𝟙 J₂) ?_).symm
103	    simp
104	  have hhg_eq_h₂ : h ≫ g = h₂ := by
105	    refine (hh₂_unique (h ≫ g) ?_).symm
106	    rw [Category.assoc, hh_eq, hg_eq]
107	  have hhg : h ≫ g = 𝟙 J₂ := by rw [hhg_eq_h₂, h₂_eq_id]
108	  use g
109	  constructor
110	  · exact hg_eq
111	  · intro g' hg'
112	    exact (hg_unique g' hg').symm
113	
114	end IsAlbanese
115	
116	/-- The identity morphism on `Spec k` is geometrically irreducible.
117	This is a small helper needed for the genus-0 case of `Jacobian`. -/
118	lemma geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec (k : Type u) [Field k] :
119	    GeometricallyIrreducible (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))) := by
120	  constructor
121	  intro x K _ y Z fst snd h
122	  have : IsIso snd := h.isIso_snd_of_isIso
123	  rw [ObjectProperty.prop_iff_of_iso (IrreducibleSpace ·) (asIso snd)]
124	  infer_instance
125	
126	/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
127	`C`: a candidate group scheme `J` (with proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible
128	structure, smooth of relative dimension `genus C`), and a proof that `J` satisfies
129	the Albanese universal property of Definition `IsAlbanese` for `C` *for every choice
130	of $k$-rational marked point* `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`.
131	
132	Mathematically, the underlying scheme `J` (and its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
133	structure) is intrinsic to `C`; only the universal morphism `C ⟶ J` depends on the
134	choice of marked point `P`. Encoding `isAlbaneseFor` as a `∀ P, IsAlbanese …` field
135	keeps the bundle independent of any specific pointing, which is what
136	`AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P` needs in order to project the Albanese morphism for
137	an *arbitrary* input `P`.
138	
139	The smoothness witness is recorded at the specific relative dimension `genus C`,
140	which is the form required by `Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`. -/
141	structure JacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
142	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
143	  /-- The candidate Albanese object. -/
144	  J : Over (Spec (.of k))
145	  /-- The group-scheme structure on `J`. -/
146	  grpObj : GrpObj J
147	  /-- `J` is proper over `k`. -/
148	  proper : IsProper J.hom
149	  /-- `J` is smooth over `k`. -/
150	  smooth : Smooth J.hom
151	  /-- `J` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
152	  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom
153	  /-- `J` is smooth of relative dimension `genus C` (so dim `J = genus C`). -/
154	  smoothGenus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) J.hom
155	  /-- The Albanese universal-property data: for every choice of $k$-rational marked
156	  point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`, the scheme `J` together with its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
157	  structure satisfies the Albanese universal property for the pointed curve `(C, P)`. -/
158	  isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
159	
160	/-- Existence of an Albanese witness for every smooth proper geometrically irreducible
161	curve. This is the single remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C Jacobian
162	scaffolding: it packages all five protected sorries (`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`,
163	`smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`)
164	into a single existence hypothesis, *and* records that the Albanese property of the
165	underlying scheme `J` is uniform over every choice of $k$-rational marked point
166	`P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C` (via the `isAlbaneseFor` field of `JacobianWitness`).
167	
168	Mathematically this corresponds to the construction of the Albanese variety of `C`,
169	classically via symmetric powers $\mathrm{Sym}^g(C)$ and the Abel--Jacobi map
170	(Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch), or equivalently as the identity component of the
171	Picard scheme. Both routes require infrastructure not yet available in Mathlib
172	(quotients of schemes by finite group actions; FGA representability), so the
173	existence is assumed and the proof is deferred to a future iteration. -/
174	theorem nonempty_jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
175	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
176	    Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) :=
177	  sorry
178	
179	/-- A choice of Albanese witness for `C`, extracted via `Classical.choice`.
180	Used to define `Jacobian C` and to discharge each of the four protected instances
181	on it. -/
182	noncomputable def jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
183	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
184	    JacobianWitness C :=
185	  Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)
186	
187	-- data
188	/-- The Jacobian of a smooth, proper curve over a field `k`.
189	
190	The Jacobian is the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness for `C` (see
191	`JacobianWitness` and `nonempty_jacobianWitness`); the existence of such a
192	witness is the single remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C scaffolding.
193	The genus-`0` specialisation is implicit in the witness — a smooth proper
194	geometrically irreducible group scheme over `k` of relative dimension `0` is
195	`Spec k` — and the genus-`0` rigidity content is absorbed into
196	`nonempty_jacobianWitness`. -/
197	noncomputable def Jacobian (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
198	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
199	  (jacobianWitness C).J
200	
201	namespace Jacobian
202	
203	/-! ## The Jacobian of `C` is an abelian variety. -/
204	
205	-- data
206	/-- The group scheme structure on the Jacobian of the curve `C`. -/
207	instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (Jacobian C) := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
208	
209	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is smooth of relative dimension `g` over `k`, where `g` is the
210	genus of `C`. -/
211	instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (Jacobian C).hom :=
212	  (jacobianWitness C).smoothGenus
213	
214	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is proper over `k`. -/
215	instance instIsProper : IsProper (Jacobian C).hom := (jacobianWitness C).proper
216	
217	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
218	instance instGeometricallyIrreducible : GeometricallyIrreducible (Jacobian C).hom :=
219	  (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
220	
221	end Jacobian
222	
223	end AlgebraicGeometry

open CategoryTheory Limits CommRingCat

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

/-- A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically `P` if for any field `K` and
morphism `Spec K ⟶ Y`, the base change `X ×[Y] Spec K` satisfies `P`. -/
def geometrically (P : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u}) : MorphismProperty Scheme.{u} :=
  fun X Y f ↦ ∀ ⦃K : Type u⦄ [Field K] (y : Spec (.of K) ⟶ Y)
    ⦃Z : Scheme.{u}⦄ (fst : Z ⟶ X) (snd : Z ⟶ Spec (.of K)),
    IsPullback fst snd f y → P Z

lemma geometrically_eq_universally (P : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u}) :
    geometrically P = .universally fun X Y _ ↦ IsIntegral Y → Subsingleton Y → P X := by
  ext X Y f
  refine ⟨fun hf Z W snd q fst h _ _ ↦ ?_, fun hf aK y Z W fst snd h ↦ ?_⟩
  · let := (isField_of_isIntegral_of_subsingleton W).toField
    apply hf (W.isoSpec.inv ≫ q) snd (fst ≫ W.isoSpec.hom)
    apply h.flip.of_iso (.refl _) (.refl _) W.isoSpec (.refl _) <;> simp
  · exact hf _ _ _ h.flip inferInstance inferInstance

lemma geometrically_inf (P Q : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u}) :
    geometrically (P ⊓ Q) = geometrically P ⊓ geometrically Q := by
  simp only [geometrically_eq_universally, ← MorphismProperty.universally_inf]
  congr with X Y f
  exact ⟨fun H ↦ ⟨(H · · |>.1), (H · · |>.2)⟩, fun H a b ↦ ⟨H.1 a b, H.2 a b⟩⟩

variable (P : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u})

instance : (geometrically P).IsStableUnderBaseChange := by
  rw [geometrically_eq_universally]
  infer_instance

Names that are made available with `open` are visible within the current `section` or `namespace`
block. This makes referring to (type) definitions and theorems easier, but note that it can also
make [scoped instances], notations, and attributes from a different namespace available.

The `open` command can be used in a few different ways:

* `open Some.Namespace.Path1 Some.Namespace.Path2` makes all non-protected names in
  `Some.Namespace.Path1` and `Some.Namespace.Path2` available without the prefix, so that
  `Some.Namespace.Path1.x` and `Some.Namespace.Path2.y` can be referred to by writing only `x` and
  `y`.

* `open Some.Namespace.Path hiding def1 def2` opens all non-protected names in `Some.Namespace.Path`
  except `def1` and `def2`.

* `open Some.Namespace.Path (def1 def2)` only makes `Some.Namespace.Path.def1` and
  `Some.Namespace.Path.def2` available without the full prefix, so `Some.Namespace.Path.def3` would
  be unaffected.

  This works even if `def1` and `def2` are `protected`.

* `open Some.Namespace.Path renaming def1 → def1', def2 → def2'` same as `open Some.Namespace.Path
  (def1 def2)` but `def1`/`def2`'s names are changed to `def1'`/`def2'`.

  This works even if `def1` and `def2` are `protected`.

* `open scoped Some.Namespace.Path1 Some.Namespace.Path2` **only** opens [scoped instances],
  notations, and attributes from `Namespace1` and `Namespace2`; it does **not** make any other name
  available.

* `open <any of the open shapes above> in` makes the names `open`-ed visible only in the next
  command or expression.

/-- SKI combinators https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SKI_combinator_calculus -/
namespace Combinator.Calculus
  def I (a : α) : α := a
  def K (a : α) : β → α := fun _ => a
  def S (x : α → β → γ) (y : α → β) (z : α) : γ := x z (y z)
end Combinator.Calculus

section
  -- open everything under `Combinator.Calculus`, *i.e.* `I`, `K` and `S`,
  -- until the section ends
  open Combinator.Calculus

  theorem SKx_eq_K : S K x = I := rfl
end

-- open everything under `Combinator.Calculus` only for the next command (the next `theorem`, here)
open Combinator.Calculus in
theorem SKx_eq_K' : S K x = I := rfl

section
  -- open only `S` and `K` under `Combinator.Calculus`
  open Combinator.Calculus (S K)

  theorem SKxy_eq_y : S K x y = y := rfl

  -- `I` is not in scope, we have to use its full path
  theorem SKxy_eq_Iy : S K x y = Combinator.Calculus.I y := rfl
end

section
  open Combinator.Calculus
    renaming
      I → identity,
      K → konstant

  #check identity
  #check konstant
end

section
  open Combinator.Calculus
    hiding S

  #check I
  #check K
end

/-! # Properties of objects which are closed under isomorphisms

Given a category `C` and `P : ObjectProperty C` (i.e. `P : C → Prop`),
this file introduces the type class `P.IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms`.

-/

@[expose] public section

universe v v' u u'

namespace CategoryTheory

variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u'} [Category.{v'} D]
  (P Q : ObjectProperty C)

namespace ObjectProperty

/-- A predicate `C → Prop` on the objects of a category is closed under isomorphisms
if whenever `P X`, then all the objects `Y` that are isomorphic to `X` also satisfy `P Y`. -/
class IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms : Prop where
  of_iso {X Y : C} (_ : X ≅ Y) (_ : P X) : P Y

lemma prop_of_iso [IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms P] {X Y : C} (e : X ≅ Y) (hX : P X) : P Y :=
  IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms.of_iso e hX

lemma prop_iff_of_iso [IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms P] {X Y : C} (e : X ≅ Y) : P X ↔ P Y :=
  ⟨prop_of_iso P e, prop_of_iso P e.symm⟩

lemma prop_of_isIso [IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms P] {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsIso f] (hX : P X) :
    P Y :=
  prop_of_iso P (asIso f) hX

lemma prop_iff_of_isIso [IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms P] {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsIso f] : P X ↔ P Y :=
  prop_iff_of_iso P (asIso f)

/-- The closure by isomorphisms of a predicate on objects in a category. -/
def isoClosure : ObjectProperty C := fun X => ∃ (Y : C) (_ : P Y), Nonempty (X ≅ Y)

lemma prop_isoClosure_iff (X : C) :
    isoClosure P X ↔ ∃ (Y : C) (_ : P Y), Nonempty (X ≅ Y) := by rfl

variable {P} in
lemma prop_isoClosure {X Y : C} (h : P X) (e : X ⟶ Y) [IsIso e] : isoClosure P Y :=
  ⟨X, h, ⟨(asIso e).symm⟩⟩

lemma le_isoClosure : P ≤ isoClosure P :=
  fun X hX => ⟨X, hX, ⟨Iso.refl X⟩⟩

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus

8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The Jacobian is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness
16	(`JacobianWitness`) for the curve `C`. Existence of such a witness is the single
17	remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C scaffolding (`nonempty_jacobianWitness`);
18	the four protected instances on `Jacobian C` all project from the witness directly.
19	
20	The file contains:
21	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
22	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
23	- `JacobianWitness`: a bundled candidate Albanese object together with the universal
24	  property uniformly over the choice of $k$-rational marked point.
25	- `nonempty_jacobianWitness`: the (deferred) existence of such a witness — the single
26	  Phase-C sorry, absorbing both higher-genus Albanese existence and genus-`0` rigidity.
27	- `Jacobian` and its four protected instances, each obtained by projection from the
28	  Albanese witness.

30	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
31	
32	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
33	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
34	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
35	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
36	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`. The witness-based definition avoids this
37	issue: in genus `0` the witness's underlying scheme `J` is `Spec k`, and in genus `> 0`
38	the witness's `J` is the honest Albanese object.
39	-/

45	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
46	
47	namespace AlgebraicGeometry

49	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
50	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
51	  [IsProper C.hom]
52	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
53	
54	/-- The Albanese universal property for a pointed smooth proper curve `(C, P)`.
55	An object `J` (with abelian-variety structure) is the Albanese of `(C, P)` if it
56	receives a universal pointed morphism `C ⟶ J` sending `P` to the identity. -/
57	def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
58	    (J : Over (Spec (.of k)))
59	    [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop :=
60	  ∃ α : C ⟶ J, P ≫ α = η[J] ∧
61	    ∀ {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
62	      [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (_ : P ≫ f = η[A]),
63	      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = α ≫ g
64	
65	namespace IsAlbanese
66	
67	noncomputable def ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
68	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
69	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J :=
70	  Classical.choose h
71	
72	lemma comp_ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
73	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
74	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) :
75	    P ≫ h.ofCurve = η[J] :=
76	  (Classical.choose_spec h).1
77	
78	lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
79	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
80	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J)
81	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
82	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
83	    ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = h.ofCurve ≫ g :=
84	  (Classical.choose_spec h).2 f hf
85	
86	/-- The Albanese object is unique up to a unique isomorphism compatible with the
87	universal morphisms. -/
88	theorem unique {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))} {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C}
89	    {J₁ J₂ : Over (Spec (.of k))}
90	    [GrpObj J₁] [IsProper J₁.hom] [Smooth J₁.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₁.hom]
91	    [GrpObj J₂] [IsProper J₂.hom] [Smooth J₂.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₂.hom]
92	    (h₁ : IsAlbanese C P J₁) (h₂ : IsAlbanese C P J₂) :
93	    ∃! (e : J₁ ⟶ J₂), h₂.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ e := by
94	  have ⟨g, hg_eq, hg_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
95	  have ⟨h, hh_eq, hh_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
96	  have ⟨g₁, hg₁_eq, hg₁_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
97	  have g₁_eq_id : g₁ = 𝟙 J₁ := by
98	    refine (hg₁_unique (𝟙 J₁) ?_).symm
99	    simp
100	  have ggh_eq_g₁ : g ≫ h = g₁ := by
101	    refine hg₁_unique (g ≫ h) ?_
102	    show h₁.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ (g ≫ h)
103	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hg_eq, ← hh_eq]
104	  have hgh : g ≫ h = 𝟙 J₁ := by rw [ggh_eq_g₁, g₁_eq_id]
105	  have ⟨k₂, hk₂_eq, hk₂_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
106	  have k₂_eq_id : k₂ = 𝟙 J₂ := by
107	    refine (hk₂_unique (𝟙 J₂) ?_).symm
108	    simp
109	  have hhg_eq_k₂ : h ≫ g = k₂ := by
110	    refine hk₂_unique (h ≫ g) ?_
111	    show h₂.ofCurve = h₂.ofCurve ≫ (h ≫ g)
112	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hh_eq, ← hg_eq]
113	  have hhg : h ≫ g = 𝟙 J₂ := by rw [hhg_eq_k₂, k₂_eq_id]
114	  exact ⟨g, hg_eq, fun g' hg' => hg_unique g' hg'⟩
115	
116	end IsAlbanese

118	/-- The identity morphism on `Spec k` is geometrically irreducible.
119	This is a small helper needed for the genus-0 case of `Jacobian`. -/
120	lemma geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec (k : Type u) [Field k] :
121	    GeometricallyIrreducible (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))) where
122	  geometrically_irreducibleSpace := by
123	    intro K _ y Z fst snd h
124	    haveI : IsIso snd := h.isIso_snd_of_isIso
125	    exact ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso (P := (IrreducibleSpace · : ObjectProperty Scheme))
126	      (asIso snd).symm inferInstance
127	
128	/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
129	`C`: a candidate group scheme `J` (with proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible
130	structure, smooth of relative dimension `genus C`), and a proof that `J` satisfies
131	the Albanese universal property of Definition `IsAlbanese` for `C` *for every choice
132	of $k$-rational marked point* `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`.
133	
134	Mathematically, the underlying scheme `J` (and its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
135	structure) is intrinsic to `C`; only the universal morphism `C ⟶ J` depends on the
136	choice of marked point `P`. Encoding `isAlbaneseFor` as a `∀ P, IsAlbanese …` field
137	keeps the bundle independent of any specific pointing, which is what
138	`AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P` needs in order to project the Albanese morphism for
139	an *arbitrary* input `P`.
140	
141	The smoothness witness is recorded at the specific relative dimension `genus C`,
142	which is the form required by `Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`. -/
143	structure JacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
144	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
145	  /-- The candidate Albanese object. -/
146	  J : Over (Spec (.of k))
147	  /-- The group-scheme structure on `J`. -/
148	  grpObj : GrpObj J
149	  /-- `J` is proper over `k`. -/
150	  proper : IsProper J.hom
151	  /-- `J` is smooth over `k`. -/
152	  smooth : Smooth J.hom
153	  /-- `J` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
154	  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom
155	  /-- `J` is smooth of relative dimension `genus C` (so dim `J = genus C`). -/
156	  smoothGenus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) J.hom
157	  /-- The Albanese universal-property data: for every choice of $k$-rational marked
158	  point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`, the scheme `J` together with its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
159	  structure satisfies the Albanese universal property for the pointed curve `(C, P)`. -/
160	  isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
161	
162	/-- Existence of an Albanese witness for every smooth proper geometrically irreducible
163	curve. This is the single remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C Jacobian
164	scaffolding: it packages all five protected sorries (`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`,
165	`smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`)
166	into a single existence hypothesis, *and* records that the Albanese property of the
167	underlying scheme `J` is uniform over every choice of $k$-rational marked point
168	`P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C` (via the `isAlbaneseFor` field of `JacobianWitness`).
169	
170	Mathematically this corresponds to the construction of the Albanese variety of `C`,
171	classically via symmetric powers $\mathrm{Sym}^g(C)$ and the Abel--Jacobi map
172	(Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch), or equivalently as the identity component of the
173	Picard scheme. Both routes require infrastructure not yet available in Mathlib
174	(quotients of schemes by finite group actions; FGA representability), so the
175	existence is assumed and the proof is deferred to a future iteration. -/
176	theorem nonempty_jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
177	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
178	    Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) :=
179	  sorry
180	
181	/-- A choice of Albanese witness for `C`, extracted via `Classical.choice`.
182	Used to define `Jacobian C` and to discharge each of the four protected instances
183	on it. -/
184	noncomputable def jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
185	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
186	    JacobianWitness C :=
187	  Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)
188	
189	-- data
190	/-- The Jacobian of a smooth, proper curve over a field `k`.
191	
192	The Jacobian is the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness for `C` (see
193	`JacobianWitness` and `nonempty_jacobianWitness`); the existence of such a
194	witness is the single remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C scaffolding.
195	The genus-`0` specialisation is implicit in the witness — a smooth proper
196	geometrically irreducible group scheme over `k` of relative dimension `0` is
197	`Spec k` — and the genus-`0` rigidity content is absorbed into
198	`nonempty_jacobianWitness`. -/
199	noncomputable def Jacobian (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
200	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
201	  (jacobianWitness C).J
202	
203	namespace Jacobian
204	
205	/-! ## The Jacobian of `C` is an abelian variety. -/
206	
207	-- data
208	/-- The group scheme structure on the Jacobian of the curve `C`. -/
209	noncomputable instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (Jacobian C) := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
210	
211	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is smooth of relative dimension `g` over `k`, where `g` is the
212	genus of `C`. -/
213	instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (Jacobian C).hom :=
214	  (jacobianWitness C).smoothGenus
215	
216	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is proper over `k`. -/
217	instance instIsProper : IsProper (Jacobian C).hom := (jacobianWitness C).proper
218	
219	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
220	instance instGeometricallyIrreducible : GeometricallyIrreducible (Jacobian C).hom :=
221	  (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
222	
223	end Jacobian
224	
225	end AlgebraicGeometry

# AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean — iter-077 Lane 3 REPAIR

**Result:** RESOLVED. File compiles cleanly (no errors). Sorry count: 1 → 1 (the single
`nonempty_jacobianWitness` sorry at L179 preserved). AbelJacobi.lean now also compiles
cleanly (cascade resolved).

## Errors repaired (6 → 0)

### L207 / noncomputable modifier
- **Approach:** Added `noncomputable` to `instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (Jacobian C) := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED. The three Prop-valued instances (`smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`) do NOT need `noncomputable` — only `instGrpObj` (which is data, since `GrpObj` is a data-carrying class) needs it. Verified by recompiling.

### L121 / intro pattern mismatch in `geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec`
- **Approach 1 (initial):** Replaced the `constructor; intro x K _ y Z fst snd h` pattern with the structure-field syntax `GeometricallyIrreducible … where geometrically_irreducibleSpace := by intro K _ y Z fst snd h`. Removed one extra `intro` (the original code had 8 binders but the field type only has 7).
- **Issue:** The downstream `rw [ObjectProperty.prop_iff_of_iso (IrreducibleSpace ·) (asIso snd)]` then failed with `failed to synthesize TopologicalSpace x✝` because `(IrreducibleSpace ·)` couldn't be typed without an explicit `P : ObjectProperty Scheme` annotation.
- **Approach 2 (final):** Replaced `rw + infer_instance` with a direct `ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso` application carrying the explicit type annotation:
  ```lean
  exact ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso (P := (IrreducibleSpace · : ObjectProperty Scheme))
    (asIso snd).symm inferInstance
  ```
  The `.symm` is needed because `snd : Z ⟶ Spec K`, but we want to transfer `IrreducibleSpace (Spec K)` (the existing Mathlib instance for `Spec` of a field) to `IrreducibleSpace Z`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED.

### L57/L59/L67/L71 / `IsAlbanese` parser cascade + Field auto-binding
This was the trickiest. The **root cause** is NOT what the plan agent's hint guessed (variable propagation through namespace): it's that **`ι` is a Mathlib scoped notation** for `GrpObj.inv`, declared in `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_`:

```lean
@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "ι" => GrpObj.inv
```

Since our file opens `MonObj` (where this scoped notation lives), the binder `(ι : C ⟶ J)` in `IsAlbanese`'s body is reinterpreted as the notation, causing `unexpected token 'ι'; expected '_' or identifier`.

- **Fix 1 (`IsAlbanese`):** Renamed the existential binder `ι` to `α` (still mathematically meaningful — `α : C ⟶ J` for the universal Albanese morphism). Also changed `(P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C)` to `(P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)` (mirror of `AbelJacobi.lean`'s explicit form) and renamed the second-conjunct hypothesis `(hf : ...)` to `(_ : ...)` since it's only used by `∃!`.
- **Fix 2 (`namespace IsAlbanese` declarations):** Added explicit `{k : Type u} [Field k]` binders to `ofCurve`, `comp_ofCurve`, `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`, and `unique`. The plan agent's hint was correct that the outer `variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over ...) ...` is not propagating into the namespace; the explicit binders fix the `Field ?m.38` stuck errors at L67/L71.
- **Result:** RESOLVED. All five `IsAlbanese`-related errors gone.

### L90/L91 / cascade from L57-L71
- **Result:** RESOLVED automatically once L57/L67/L71 were fixed.

### L101/L109/L113 / `Eq.symm` direction in `unique`
After the rest compiled, three new errors surfaced from the existing `unique` proof body (these were latent under the L57-L71 cascade and not previously visible):

- `hg₁_unique (g ≫ h) ?_ : g ≫ h = g₁` (NOT `g₁ = g ≫ h`). So `.symm` was reversing in the wrong direction. **Fix:** removed the three offending `.symm`s on L101, L109, L113.
- Additionally the `rw [Category.assoc, hg_eq, hh_eq]` was rewriting in the wrong direction — the unbeta-reduced goal had `h₁.ofCurve ≫ (g ≫ h)`, not `(_ ≫ _) ≫ _`. **Fix:** added `change h₁.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ (g ≫ h)` (beta-reduces the lambda from `∃!` unfolding) and used `rw [← Category.assoc, ← hg_eq, ← hh_eq]`.
- **Variable shadowing**: the inner `have ⟨h₂, hh₂_eq, hh₂_unique⟩ := ...` shadowed the outer `h₂ : IsAlbanese C P J₂`. After the shadowing, `h₂.ofCurve` no longer resolved. **Fix:** renamed the inner morphism to `k₂` (with `hk₂_eq`, `hk₂_unique`, `hhg_eq_k₂`, `k₂_eq_id`).
- Switched the final `use g; refine ⟨..., ?_⟩; intro; exact ...` to a direct `exact ⟨g, hg_eq, fun g' hg' => hg_unique g' hg'⟩` to avoid `use`'s auto-closing behaviour.
- **Result:** RESOLVED.

## Protected signatures preserved

All five protected declarations are byte-for-byte unchanged at the signature level. The only body-level changes:
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian` (L199–201): unchanged.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian.instGrpObj` (L209): added `noncomputable` prefix only (allowed per `archon-protected.yaml` — body-level change).
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus` (L213): unchanged.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian.instIsProper` (L217): unchanged.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian.instGeometricallyIrreducible` (L220): unchanged.

(The protected declarations in `AbelJacobi.lean` — `ofCurve`, `comp_ofCurve`, `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` — were not touched.)

## Final state
